2606.22033.title: A Completion-Aware Framework for Impactful Counterfactual Explainability in Graph Neural Networks
2606.22033.paper_summary: In this study, we propose a novel pipeline for generic, model-agnostic, local-level counterfactual explainability in graph neural networks (GNNs). Although counterfactual explainers capable of both adding and removing edges have emerged in recent years, the need for generic and efficient solutions remains unmet, particularly concerning qualitative explanation generation. Our approach couples progress in factual explainability with missing edge prediction models rooted in link prediction research, in order to enhance the quality, robustness and intuitiveness of explanations. A multi-faceted experimental analysis conducted on real-world and synthetic graph classification benchmarks, both binary and multi-label, demonstrates the advancements in comparison to state-of-the-art baselines across diverse metrics.
2606.22033.paper_summary_zh: 在這項研究中，我們提出了一個新穎的管道，用於圖神經網絡（GNNs）中的通用、模型無關的局部反事實可解釋性。儘管近年來已出現能夠同時添加和移除邊的反事實解釋器，但對於通用和高效解決方案的需求仍未得到滿足，特別是在質性解釋生成方面。我們的方法將事實可解釋性的進展與根植於鏈接預測研究的缺失邊預測模型相結合，以提高解釋的質量、穩健性和直觀性。在對真實世界和合成圖分類基準進行的多方面實驗分析中，無論是二元還是多標籤，均顯示出與最先進基準相比的進步，涵蓋多種指標。
2606.22033.author: Maria Myrto Villia et.al.
2606.22033.authors: Maria Myrto Villia, Filippos Gouidis, Theodore Patkos, Panos Trahanias
